question: Are the provisions of a bilateral investment treaty concluded between two Member States, which confer jurisdiction to resolve investor-State disputes upon an arbitral tribunal that is not part of the EU judicial system, compatible with EU law?
topic: Autonomy of EU law — intra-EU bilateral investment treaty — investor-State arbitration (Arts 267 and 344 TFEU)
gold_answer: No. Articles 267 and 344 TFEU preclude a provision in an agreement between Member States (such as Article 8 of the BIT) under which an investor of one Member State may, in a dispute concerning investments in the other, bring proceedings against that State before an arbitral tribunal whose jurisdiction it has undertaken to accept. Such a clause removes disputes that may concern the interpretation/application of EU law from the EU judicial system, undermines mutual trust and the preliminary-ruling procedure (Art. 267 TFEU), and adversely affects the autonomy of EU law (Achmea, C-284/16, paras 58-60).
notes: corpus_gap: Achmea (C-284/16, Grand Chamber, decided 2018 but CELEX year 2016) is ABSENT from BOTH corpora today; exists in CELLAR with English text, in the 2016 JUDG list. Gold paragraphs 58-60. The v2 backfill of year 2016 should capture it.
